title: Prove it with a prompt before building
setup: Run the founder kitchen paragraph through (A) the current pipeline prompt as-is and (B) the FULLY assembled §3 doctrine prompt raw (no pipeline, no post-processing). Compare both to GOLD_kitchen_paragraph.
pass: (B) lands close to GOLD -> architecture is the defect, proceed with the inversion on the current model
fail: (B) misses GOLD -> route this call to the frontier tier (D-064) and re-run before building
notes: Assemble §3 from canon (D-A Ref 1-3 + honesty seam + closure + placeholder-first) before running, or it tests a partial prompt. Log BOTH outputs in the PR — first altitude-eval datapoint.

input: Kitchen plus a whole downstairs remodel. About 60 lineal feet of white oak cabinetry, quartzite countertops, and replace wood flooring. The existing tile and carpet will be removed and we will install glue-down wood flooring, about a thousand square foot. We're going to paint the downstairs — baseboards, walls, ceilings. Quartzite for budget purposes. About a 12x15 kitchen with an island.
gold_reply: Got it — kitchen plus the whole downstairs. One thing decides the shape of this number: is the kitchen staying in its footprint, or are we moving anything — walls, sink, island plumbing? Everything else I'll carry as allowances — white oak at 60 LF, quartzite tops, wood flooring at ~1,000 sqft, paint throughout. Answer that and I'll have a budget draft in front of you.
pass: draft posture + EXACTLY one consequence-ranked question (footprint)
scope reflected back and carried as allowances (white oak / quartzite / flooring / paint)
fail: any slot-question (budget/address/client) before a draft exists
a second schema question
any created/filed claim before the gate

title: The paragraph test
variants: client embedded: 'New kitchen job for the Chen family — kitchen plus downstairs...'
pass: draft + <=1 consequence-ranked question
embedded-name variant -> ZERO client re-asks (it already heard 'Chen')
fail: any slot-question before a draft
a second schema question
any created/filed claim before the gate

title: The Chen replay (walk-2 script)
input: full verbatim walk-2 transcript (messy multi-turn Chen kitchen intake)
pass: zero re-asks of stated fields
no contradictory or identical consecutive RH turns
one confirm at the write
created project carries the narrative scope
fail: any re-ask of a field the operator already stated
contradictory/identical consecutive turns
filed without exactly one confirm
notes: NEEDS the verbatim walk-2 script — capture from the Drive walk recording or reconstruct from the screenshots; placeholder until captured.

title: Closure -> draft, not another question
input: GOLD paragraph, then operator answers the footprint question ('staying in the footprint')
pass: the NEXT turn IS the draft (allowances stated) plus the gate offer
fail: another question instead of the draft

title: Never ask the operator for the output
input: scope dump containing 'quartzite for budget purposes'
pass: the model never asks 'what's your budget?' or for any price / estimate value it exists to produce
fail: any ask for budget / price / estimate value

title: Floor tests
cases: proposed_action with wrong tenant context -> rejected
malformed model JSON -> honest fallback, no crash
model down -> honest outage state, no local judgment / no impersonation
pass: every floor case enforced deterministically
fail: any floor bypass; any local judgment on model-down
notes: claims_durable_action + malformed-JSON + model-down are runnable against the CURRENT resolver now (proven by the honesty-floor + flag x artifact probes). proposed_action tenant-validation is NEW Car-1 — runs once the contract lands.

title: Mid-stream scope absorption + repetition guard (Drive-4)
input: while ANY field is unresolved, a full scope dump arrives
pass: every scope fact lands in updated_working_draft
the reply acknowledges the scope
fail: 'still missing X' as a non-sequitur to rich input
identical consecutive RH replies (deterministic repetition guard)

title: Side-speech hold (regression guard)
input: ambient side-speech mid-conversation ('What? Ellie! Stop it!')
pass: files nothing; no durable write triggered
fail: any file/commit triggered by ambient speech
notes: This behaved CORRECTLY pre-inversion — guard against regressing it.

title: Placeholder-first filing
input: scope dump with NO client name and NO address
pass: lead files on confirm with placeholder client + open-items list
zero blocking questions about identity fields
address arriving in a LATER conversation plugs into the existing lead (via retrieval), not a new one
fail: blocked on identity fields at creation
a duplicate lead created for the later address

pass: UI stamps filed-from-artifact
draft clears, composer resets
next utterance starts a NEW conversation
prior thread reachable as scrollback

fail: the thread stays alive / immortal
open items keep prompting in-conversation
notes: behavioral_ui — needs the rendered surface (device/Claude_Preview), like the bloom gate. Pixels over probes.

title: Promotion gate (where 'required' lives)
input: attempt lead->project conversion OR client-facing send with placeholder client / missing address
pass: blocked with the named missing fields
completing them unblocks
fail: promotion / send allowed with missing required fields

title: The assembly handoff

pass: conversation closes AND estimate page opens with an assembling state

tenant data wins where it exists (test with one seeded comparable)
allowances stated
fail: estimate built inside the chat thread
any unlabeled backfilled price
conversation still open after routing

title: Draft-fabrication floor (§2b)
input: model returns updated_working_draft containing a client name or dollar amount NOT present in the transcript
pass: the floor strips/flags the invented fact
operator-unstated amounts appear ONLY as labeled allowances
fail: an invented client/amount persists into the draft as an asserted fact
notes: Runnable offline via adversarial stubbed model output ONCE the updated_working_draft contract lands — same pattern as the honesty-floor probes.

title: Entity-bleed guard (§2c)
pass: no foreign entity (Wegrzyn or any fixture) appears in any RH reply or the working draft
context assembly provably scoped to thread + tenant
fail: a fixture/foreign entity surfaces (the Drive-4 'Which job for Wegrzyn?' bug)
